title: Captivate Digital AI | Darnell Disroe
description: Captivate Digital AI is the SEO and lead-generation platform led by Darnell Disroe for local growth and conversion-focused publishing.
headline: Captivate Digital AI
intro: Captivate Digital AI is the search and conversion system presented by Darnell Disroe through Captivate Digital SEO.

sections.title: Search Visibility
sections.body: Captivate Digital SEO uses structured pages and clear metadata to help prospects find the business and understand its services quickly.

sections.title: Lead Capture
sections.body: The site routes inbound interest into the embedded CRM workflow so Darnell Disroe can follow up through a controlled lead pipeline.

sections.title: Brand Clarity
sections.body: The site now presents Captivate Digital SEO and Darnell Disroe together so the business and representative are aligned across content and schema.

title: About Darnell Disroe | Captivate Digital SEO
description: Learn how Darnell Disroe leads Captivate Digital SEO through search strategy, structured publishing, and lead capture systems.
headline: Darnell Disroe is the named representative behind Captivate Digital SEO.
intro: This page explains who Darnell Disroe is in relation to Captivate Digital SEO and how the business approaches search visibility, publishing, and lead generation.

sections.title: Named Representation
sections.body: The site now identifies Darnell Disroe directly so visitors and search engines can connect the person and the business truthfully.

sections.title: Service Positioning
sections.body: Captivate Digital SEO is positioned as the operating brand while Darnell Disroe is presented as the founder or public-facing representative.

sections.title: Search Consistency
sections.body: Page copy, metadata, and schema now reinforce the same relationship between the business brand and Darnell Disroe.

title: Publishing Services
description: A neutral example services page for content systems, performance optimization, and static deployment workflows.
headline: Use one build pipeline for content generation, optimization, and deployment packaging.
intro: This page shows how a services route can reuse the same layout and page model while exposing different content.

sections.title: Content Modeling
sections.body: Keep route-specific copy in data files and let the build system render consistent pages automatically.

sections.title: Metadata Hygiene
sections.body: Generate sitemap, robots, and structured data from the same route list to avoid inconsistencies.

sections.title: Static Delivery
sections.body: Package the built output into a dist folder that can be dropped onto common static hosts without extra steps.

title: Contact
description: Contact Captivate Digital SEO and Darnell Disroe through the embedded CRM intake form.
headline: Contact Darnell Disroe through Captivate Digital SEO.
intro: Use this route to reach Darnell Disroe through the Captivate Digital SEO CRM workflow for search, publishing, and lead-generation discussions.

form.heading: Request a conversation
form.description: Use the embedded GHL CRM form to route qualified inquiries directly into your lead pipeline.
form.successMessage: After submission, GHL can redirect to your controlled success or error route.

sections.title: Lead Intake
sections.body: The embedded CRM form routes inquiry details directly into the Captivate Digital SEO follow-up workflow managed by Darnell Disroe.

sections.title: Response Path
sections.body: Success and error redirects keep the user on a first-party route while the business handles next steps behind the form submission.

sections.title: Business Contact
sections.body: This page is the direct contact path for prospects who want to work with Captivate Digital SEO and Darnell Disroe.

title: Inquiry Received
description: Confirmation page for successful contact form submissions.
headline: Your inquiry has been received and routed to the configured intake flow.
intro: This page is intended to be used as the success redirect target for your hosted form provider.

sections.title: Submission Complete
sections.body: The hosted provider accepted the submission and redirected the visitor back to a controlled first-party route.

sections.title: Operational Continuity
sections.body: A dedicated success route lets the site keep a consistent experience after form handoff without requiring a custom backend.

sections.title: Next Step
sections.body: Use this page for follow-up expectations, routing details, or conversion tracking tags.

title: Submission Problem
description: Fallback page for contact form submission issues.
headline: The provider could not complete the submission with the current request.
intro: This route acts as a controlled error redirect so the user stays on a first-party page even if provider validation fails.

sections.title: Recover Cleanly
sections.body: Send the visitor back to the form or provide alternate contact instructions when the provider rejects the submission.

sections.title: Preserve Trust
sections.body: An explicit error route prevents the submission flow from ending on a generic third-party error page.

sections.title: Debug Later
sections.body: If needed, add analytics or logging cues here to identify common provider-side failure patterns.

title: FAQ
description: Common questions about the site architecture, build pipeline, and deployment workflow.
headline: Answers to the questions that usually come up once a static site starts growing.
intro: This route shows how the same template can support another content page without any manual HTML duplication.

sections.title: How are pages created?
sections.body: Pages are generated from structured content data and one shared template, so layout changes apply everywhere at once.

sections.title: How do I add a route?
sections.body: Add a new object to the content file, rerun the build, and the navigation, sitemap, schema, and output pages update automatically.

sections.title: What gets deployed?
sections.body: The public site is processed, optimized, and then copied into dist, which is the folder intended for static hosting.

title: Case Study: Platform Rollout
description: A specialized case-study page type for implementation narratives, metrics, and phased delivery notes.
headline: A phased rollout story can live inside the same publishing system without falling back to ad hoc HTML.
intro: This route demonstrates a dedicated case-study renderer with client metadata, metrics, a phase timeline, and narrative body content.

caseStudy.quote: The structured rollout let us ship a cleaner content system without taking on a custom application stack.

phases.title: Content Inventory
phases.body: The existing route set was normalized into a single structured content inventory before any visual migration work began.

phases.title: Template Mapping
phases.body: Pages were grouped by page type so a small number of reusable renderers could replace one-off HTML and duplicated metadata.

phases.title: Governed Launch
phases.body: Validation, preview, and deployment packaging were run through the same pipeline to reduce release drift.

sections.title: Business Outcome
sections.body: The case-study format captures implementation detail while still fitting inside the static publishing pipeline used by every other page.

sections.title: Template Reuse
sections.body: A dedicated renderer handles narrative structure, but the route still inherits navigation, schema generation, sitemap updates, and asset optimization.

sections.title: Operational Fit
sections.body: This pattern works well for delivery summaries, implementation retrospectives, and client-facing project writeups.

title: Operational Resources
description: A reusable long-form page type for guides, playbooks, and structured operational resources.
headline: A fourth template type can support operational content without breaking the shared system.
intro: This route demonstrates a dedicated resource template for longer guidance, lightweight metrics, and reusable highlight cards.

highlights.title: Long-form Friendly
highlights.body: Markdown-backed body copy can grow without making the route definition noisy or difficult to scan.

highlights.title: Operational Metadata
highlights.body: Small metrics and highlight cards make it easier to present structured implementation notes alongside narrative content.

highlights.title: Reusable Surface
highlights.body: The new template proves the dispatcher can support additional page types without changing the broader build pipeline.

sections.title: Use Cases
sections.body: Use this page type for guides, policy pages, implementation notes, or internal playbooks that need more structure than a basic marketing page.

sections.title: Shared Delivery
sections.body: Even with a new layout, the route still inherits shared navigation, metadata generation, schema, minification, and packaging.

sections.title: Future Scaling
sections.body: Additional page types can be added the same way as long as they stay inside the validated content contract.
